seo.title: N. Carolina Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in North Carolina — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license north carolina, dental assistant certification north carolina, RDA north carolina, DANB CDA north carolina, dental assistant radiography north carolina

board.name: North Carolina State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (classification based)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in North Carolina.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: No state application required for general dental assistants. Check expanded function requirements.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: North Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, North Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in North Carolina.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in North Carolina. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: North Carolina classifies dental assistants (DA I, DA II, DA III) based on experience and training rather than formal licensure.
